title: Choosing the Right Chart for Your Business Question
summary: Wrong chart types confuse more than bad data. Match visual form to the question you ask.

content.summary: Wrong chart types confuse more than bad data. Match visual form to the question you ask.

body.text: Trend over time needs lines; composition needs bars; relationship exploration needs scatter—not interchangeable widgets. How much by category? Sorted bar chart. How did we trend weekly?

body.alt: Team collaborating around data on a screen
body.caption: Team collaborating around data on a screen. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: How much by category? Sorted bar chart. How did we trend weekly? Line chart on date axis. What share of total? Stacked bar or simple percent table.
Avoid pie charts with twelve slices and dual-axis charts that imply false correlations.

items: Line charts for continuous time series.
Limit categories for readability—filter long tails.

body.text: Upload, preview types, try chart, adjust filters. Wrong visual often signals wrong grain or filter scope.
Right chart plus saved rules makes monthly reviews predictable.
Datilio chart picker supports business questions without a design degree.

meta_description: Wrong chart types confuse more than bad data. Match visual form to the question you ask.
meta_keywords: Charts, Visualization, Decision Guide, Best Practices
